channel.title: healthTest
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from healthTest, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Early menarche and later menopause are associated with increased ovarian cancer risk
item.description: 1. Across the premenopausal and postmenopausal groups, early menarche, later menopause, and longer reproductive span were associated with higher ovari...

item.title: Dr. Oz pushes AI avatars as a fix for rural health care. Not so fast, critics say
item.description: Dr. Mehmet Oz, who heads the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services, is advancing a $50 billion plan to modernize rural health care.

item.title: Online medical misinformation is scarce, but older adults see most of it
item.description: Even as misinformation proliferates across the Internet, sites containing low-credibility health information remain relatively scarce and unseen.

item.title: First whole-heart mapping technology delivers a global view of cardiac arrhythmias in a single beat
item.description: Corify Care's proprietary Global Volumetric Mapping technology, described in Communications Medicine, represents a new approach to cardiac electrophys...

item.title: Malawi vaccinates a new generation of children against polio, which still hasn't been eradicated
item.description: In a classroom in southern Malawi, children sit in rows on the floor as a health worker moves among them administering an oral vaccine that protects a...

item.title: Empathic robots on future health care teams: Researchers review Pepper and NAO robot effectiveness
item.description: A child with a new cochlear implant works on auditory rehabilitation exercises at home in Toulouse, France, aided by Pepper, a human-looking robot. In...

item.title: States sue to block $600 million cut to public health funds
item.description: Four states are suing to stop the Trump administration from rescinding hundreds of millions of dollars already set aside for public health programs.

item.title: Judge blocks Trump admin from rescinding health grants to Democratic-led states
item.description: The ruling temporarily blocks the Trump Administration from cutting $600 million in public health grants that had already been allocated to four Democ...

item.title: A telephone-based intervention reduced loneliness and improved well-being among older adults
item.description: 1. Four weeks of telephone-delivered behavioral activation and mindfulness interventions by lay counsellors were associated with reduced loneliness an...

item.title: RFK Jr. made promises to get his job as health secretary. He's broken many of them
item.description: In his confirmation hearings, Robert F. Kennedy Jr. told U.S. senators that he would not cut funding for vaccine research or change the nation's offic...

item.title: Colorectal cancer is leading cancer killer for people under 50
item.description: James Van Der Beek's death at age 48 highlights a sad statistic: More people under 50 are getting colorectal cancer.

item.title: A familiar move with a new twist: Trump tries to cut CDC funds he just signed into law
item.description: A federal judge in Illinois quickly issued a restraining order after the Trump administration slashed more than $600 million in CDC grants to four blu...

item.title: The cost of clinician absence in the boardroom: a 30-year perspective
item.description: I have spent 30 years as a physical medicine and rehabilitation physician in solo practice. In three decades of treating patients, I have witnessed th...

item.title: Immigration policy and child health: a medical student’s perspective
item.description: “Do no harm.” Upon entering medical school, my classmates and I uttered these words with pride and joy, unaware of the deeper meaning behind them. Whi...

item.title: In the age of AI, what makes a physician REAL?
item.description: It was 3:17 a.m. when the trauma pager went off. “Level 1 incoming. MVC. Hypotensive.” The trauma bay shifted instantly from stillness to choreography...

item.title: 2026 Winter Olympics rumors: the truth about ski jumpers and hyaluronic acid
item.description: An excerpt from Still Life with Stethoscope and Typewriter: Essays and Echoes of Narrative Medicine. Every Olympic Games eventually produces its own m...

item.title: Benefits of TrumpRx Drug Site Are Limited, Experts Say
item.description: A new federal prescription drug site promises to save patients money on popular drugs. Here’s what physicians should know about TrumpRx. Medscape Medi...

item.title: My wife wants me to retire
item.description: At our yearly Christmas medical staff dinner this last year, our chief of staff asked all the doctors present who had been working at our hospital for...

item.title: Spending Package Extends Telehealth; PBMs Face Regulations
item.description: A recent federal appropriations bill carried with it 2-year extensions of telehealth provisions, along with changes in policy intended to set the stag...

item.title: Stigma Driving Illegal Erectile Dysfunction Pill Sales
item.description: More than 4.4 million illegal erectile dysfunction pills were seized in 2025 as the MHRA warned that stigma and embarrassment are pushing men in the U...

item.title: Rural Accountable Care Organizations: Better Diabetes Care
item.description: Participation in accountable care organizations shows better diabetes care outcomes for patients in rural areas than for those in urban settings. Meds...

item.title: Dream Orgasms: Clinical Insight or Emerging Phenomenon?
item.description: New research suggests that orgasms without genital stimulation, including during REM sleep, may reflect central neurophysiologic processes with therap...

item.title: Propofol is associated with lower mortality in sepsis-associated encephalopathy
item.description: 1. Among patients with sepsis-associated encephalopathy, propofol monotherapy was associated with lower one-year all-cause mortality compared to no se...

item.title: The Scan by 2 Minute Medicine®: Daily caffeine intake linked to lower dementia risk, AAP sues over federal vaccine schedule overhaul, Catherine O’Hara passes away after short illness, and GLP-1 receptor agonists reduce endometrial cancer risk
item.description: The Scan by 2 Minute Medicine® is a pop-culture medical newsletter and exclusive benefit for 2 Minute Medicine Plus subscribers. Moderate caffeine int...

item.title: Polypharmacy is associated with higher risk of mortality and hospitalizations among older adults
item.description: 1. Polypharmacy was associated with a higher risk of mortality and hospitalization at one-year and five-years among older adults aged >75 years. Evide...
